parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'claude-opus-4-7', max_tokens: 600, system: 'You are a data center market analyst tracking development activity and hiring signals for a power origination executive transitioning into data center development. Return ONLY valid JSON, no preamble, no markdown fences.', messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Title: ' + $json.title + '

Description: ' + $json.description + '

Source: ' + $json.source + '

Category: ' + ($json.category || 'unknown') + '
WEIGHTING: ' + ($json.category === 'energy' ? 'This is an ENERGY / power-market feed (market intelligence, not inherently a lead). Mark relevance true and score High ONLY when it implies a specific actionable opening in the wheelhouse — large-load interconnection, ISO/RTO queue, FERC Order 1000, transmission constraints, or a named developer/offtaker move. Generic policy/pricing/technology news with no named company action is NOT relevant.' : 'This is a DATA-CENTER feed (core target market). A named DC developer/operator capacity, site, power, or hiring move is high-intent; score generously when company and action are clear.') + '

Return JSON with these EXACT keys and values:

- company (string: the developer/operator/employer, not utility or recruiter)
- signal_type (MUST be exactly one of: News, FERC Filing, Permit, LinkedIn Surge, EIA Generation Spike. Map jobs and hiring announcements to LinkedIn Surge. Map PPAs and site acquisitions to News.)
- summary (string: one sentence under 180 chars; INCLUDE the MW/capacity figure and the location when they are stated in the source, e.g. 'Cipher Mining plans ~500MW data center near Waco, TX')
- state (string: US state name, or null)
- mw_size (number or null: the project MW capacity if stated ANYWHERE in the title or description; convert GW to MW (1 GW = 1000 MW) and use the numeric value for ranges/approximates, e.g. '~500MW' -> 500, '1.2 GW' -> 1200, '352 MW' -> 352; null ONLY when no capacity figure appears in the source)
- signal_score (MUST be exactly High or Medium. High = greater than 200MW, hyperscaler-anchored, FERC large-load filing, or VP/Director role at a target DC company. Medium = 50-200MW, permit, or Senior or Manager-level role.)
- outreach_angle (string under 200 chars: one-sentence hook describing why this matters to a power origination executive transitioning to data centers — focus on PPA opportunity, interconnection expertise relevance, or warm-intro path)
- actionability (integer 1-5, a DIFFERENT axis from signal_score: how reachable a DIRECT opening this is for an independent power-origination/development professional (20+ yrs, 5+ GW delivered, FERC/interconnection + PPA-structuring). A hyperscaler entering a queue is HIGH signal_score but LOW actionability. 5 = named regional developer/IPP with a clear unmet power-procurement, interconnection, or regulatory-structuring need they likely lack in-house, reachable. 4 = mid-size operator, plausible need, no deep internal origination bench. 3 = need exists but counterparty is well-resourced, or opening is indirect (partner/advisory). 2 = large operator with full internal team + named partners (market intel, not a lead). 1 = hyperscaler (AWS, Microsoft, Google, Meta, Oracle/OpenAI) or undisclosed/unreachable party. Penalize hyperscalers, Blackstone/KKR-backed deep-bench platforms, undisclosed counterparties, and pure-sentiment/opposition stories toward 1-2; reward named regional developers, BTM/CPCN and interconnection disputes, and staged multi-phase procurement toward 4-5.)
- actionability_rationale (string under 200 chars: one sentence naming the counterparty type, the unmet need or its absence, and any penalty applied)
- relevance (boolean: true ONLY if data center development, power origination, hyperscaler activity, or senior development/energy/power role at DC/hyperscaler/colo company AND would score High or Medium. Return false if would be Low.)

If relevance is false, return only {"relevance": false}.' }] }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'claude-opus-4-7', max_tokens: 600, system: 'You are a data center market analyst tracking development activity and hiring signals for a power origination executive transitioning into data center development. Return ONLY valid JSON, no preamble, no markdown fences.', messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Title: ' + $json.title + '

Description: ' + $json.description + '

Source: ' + $json.source + '

WEIGHTING: ' + ($json.category === 'energy' ? 'This is an ENERGY / power-market feed (market intelligence, not inherently a lead). Mark relevance true and score High ONLY when it implies a specific actionable opening in the wheelhouse — large-load interconnection, ISO/RTO queue, FERC Order 1000, transmission constraints, or a named developer/offtaker move. Generic policy/pricing/technology news with no named company action is NOT relevant.' : 'This is a DATA-CENTER feed (core target market). A named DC developer/operator capacity, site, power, or hiring move is high-intent; score generously when company and action are clear.') + '

- company (string: the developer/operator/employer, not utility or recruiter)
- signal_type (MUST be exactly one of: News, FERC Filing, Permit, LinkedIn Surge, EIA Generation Spike. Map jobs and hiring announcements to LinkedIn Surge. Map PPAs and site acquisitions to News.)
- summary (string: one sentence under 180 chars; INCLUDE the MW/capacity figure and the location when they are stated in the source, e.g. 'Cipher Mining plans ~500MW data center near Waco, TX')
- state (string: US state name, or null)
- mw_size (number or null: the project MW capacity if stated ANYWHERE in the title or description; convert GW to MW (1 GW = 1000 MW) and use the numeric value for ranges/approximates, e.g. '~500MW' -> 500, '1.2 GW' -> 1200, '352 MW' -> 352; null ONLY when no capacity figure appears in the source)
- signal_score (MUST be exactly High or Medium. High = greater than 200MW, hyperscaler-anchored, FERC large-load filing, or VP/Director role at a target DC company. Medium = 50-200MW, permit, or Senior or Manager-level role.)
- outreach_angle (string under 200 chars: one-sentence hook describing why this matters to a power origination executive transitioning to data centers — focus on PPA opportunity, interconnection expertise relevance, or warm-intro path)
- actionability (integer 1-5, a DIFFERENT axis from signal_score: how reachable a DIRECT opening this is for an independent power-origination/development professional (20+ yrs, 5+ GW delivered, FERC/interconnection + PPA-structuring). A hyperscaler entering a queue is HIGH signal_score but LOW actionability. 5 = named regional developer/IPP with a clear unmet power-procurement, interconnection, or regulatory-structuring need they likely lack in-house, reachable. 4 = mid-size operator, plausible need, no deep internal origination bench. 3 = need exists but counterparty is well-resourced, or opening is indirect (partner/advisory). 2 = large operator with full internal team + named partners (market intel, not a lead). 1 = hyperscaler (AWS, Microsoft, Google, Meta, Oracle/OpenAI) or undisclosed/unreachable party. Penalize hyperscalers, Blackstone/KKR-backed deep-bench platforms, undisclosed counterparties, and pure-sentiment/opposition stories toward 1-2; reward named regional developers, BTM/CPCN and interconnection disputes, and staged multi-phase procurement toward 4-5.)
- actionability_rationale (string under 200 chars: one sentence naming the counterparty type, the unmet need or its absence, and any penalty applied)
- relevance (boolean: true ONLY if data center development, power origination, hyperscaler activity, or senior development/energy/power role at DC/hyperscaler/colo company AND would score High or Medium. Return false if would be Low.)

If relevance is false, return only {"relevance": false}.' }] }) }}
